summary: Do not ship this plan yet. The committed RED proof matches red.sha and fails for the intended symptom, but the proposed fix introduces an undeclared degraded-dependency paging behavior and the planned checks do not enforce their claimed invariants. No materially simpler safe fix than a left-side rollup was found.

findings.title: RHS telemetry loss becomes a second, mislabeled paging condition
findings.body: The plan treats a 30s scrape cadence as proof that `kube_pod_container_info` is always present. During a kube-state-metrics/scrape outage, however, the RHS disappears. The rolled-up LHS remains continuous across 10m pushes, so `unless on (app, digest)` emits every app continuously and `ImageDigestDrift` fires after 20m. The current bare LHS instead holes every cycle and never fires. This creates a new, misleading per-app paging behavior distinct from actual digest drift—the same misattribution principle used to reject an LHS `absent()` guard. L2 and L3 always provide pod data, so they do not characterize this path.
findings.recommendation: Add no-pod and missing-KSM-data characterization legs. Preserve current alert behavior with an explicit current-RHS-presence guard while still allowing differently digested pods to trigger drift; otherwise declare the additional paging behavior and route it outside the single-flip bugfix.

findings.title: L3 does not enforce the claimed window or for-duration invariants
findings.body: The committed harness does not extract or compare W, and it derives `FOR` from the mutable rule rather than asserting `20m`. Its single phantom phase places the last old exporter sample 10m before BUMP and keeps the old pod visible until roughly BUMP−4.5m. Consequently W=20m produces only about 14.5m of phantom mismatch and can pass L3 despite violating `W < for`; the cited manual 30m failure only brackets 15m versus 30m. A changed `for: 15m` can also pass. The proposed characterization regex accepts any window, so an overwide window or altered paging threshold can go GREEN and introduce false pages or faster paging.
findings.recommendation: Pin `for: 20m` and the selected `15m` range in characterization, parse W/push/for and assert `push <= W < for`, and add a worst-order overwide-window witness that demonstrably fails at the upper boundary.

findings.title: The planned negative characterization never reads the rule file
findings.body: The replacement negative assertion omits `"$R"`. `grep` therefore reads stdin; under noninteractive Bats execution it normally exits nonzero at EOF, making `[ "$status" -ne 0 ]` pass regardless of the rule contents. The line described as the key strengthening is therefore vacuous and no longer checks the broken `max by (app)` form.
findings.recommendation: Pass `"$R"` to the negative grep. Prefer extracting the `app:image_digest_drift` expression with `yq` first so comments or unrelated expressions cannot satisfy either assertion.

next_steps: Revise the single-flip contract and characterization for missing RHS telemetry.
Make the W/for bounds machine-enforced rather than relying on the 15m-versus-30m observation.
Correct the proposed Bats command before implementation.
